text: I think the rebuild_state aborts because one bad line panics serde_json
Maybe we should bump task_id length from 6 to 10 chars to dodge collisions
It could be that the BufWriter is holding bytes in user space when fsync is called
Looked at db.rs:133 — rebuild_state opens an unchecked_transaction and aborts on the first parse error
Confirmed in mcp/main.rs:158 — every tool handler calls full rebuild_state, never the incremental path
In storage.rs the JsonlWriter wraps File in fd_lock::RwLock and acquires write() per append
Ran cargo test --workspace: all 193 tests green, no flakes over five runs
Bench result: rebuild_state_10k_events median 820ms, ingest_new_events 38ms — 21x faster
Reproduced the corruption: two parallel hooks racing on the same JSONL on Windows produced 7 torn lines
Going with fd-lock for the file lock — single API across Linux/macOS/Windows, well-maintained
We will return Result<Json<T>, McpError> from every tool handler instead of the success-typed envelope
Picked criterion 0.5 over divan because we already have it in transitive deps via mockito
Tried adding rwlock around index_event but it deadlocks with the open transaction — won't work
Considered storing last_event_id in a JSON sidecar but it makes the pack-cache invalidation race-prone
Anthropic API rate limit on the haiku tier is 1000 RPM per organisation
NTFS does not give us POSIX append-atomicity for writes larger than ~512 bytes
Actually the 'last_indexed_event_id is in index_state' claim was wrong — there's no row until first ingest
Correction on the earlier finding: pack-cache is invalidated per task_id, not globally
PR merged. Released 0.1.4 to crates.io. Closing this task
Shipped — task-journal doctor now runs on all three OS
Reopening — turns out the migration is missing on existing 0.1.x DBs after upgrade
This task is folded into the bigger 'v0.2.0 epic' work; closing in favour of that
This belongs under the OAuth task, not the data-storage one
Maybe the problem is FTS5 not being a regular index; could be slow at 100k events
Checked the metrics directory: only one project_hash present, no orphans
e2e: ran full pipeline 50 times, p99 < 200ms, p50 < 50ms
Decision: incremental indexing reads only events since last_indexed_event_id, falls back to full rebuild on missing marker
Won't take the rusqlite multithreaded feature — adds a c-bindgen rebuild for no real win
On Windows GitHub runners cmd.exe is required to launch .cmd shims, no PowerShell fallback
Earlier I said BufWriter was needed for perf — that was wrong, perf delta is unmeasurable for this workload
